Title: Govt focused on BUDI rollout, not T20 reclassification, says Amir Hamzah
Article:
BALIK PULAU: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the cabinet has not discussed any new classification involving the T20 income group.
He said the government's current focus is on the nationwide implementation of the BUDI fuel subsidy mechanism rather than introducing new income category classifications.
"As explained by Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil last week, the cabinet did not discuss any T-category classifications," he said.
"Our approach is to focus on the implementation of BUDI diesel nationwide. We are using the same approach as BUDI 95 as we believe the system is comprehensive and fair to all," he said.
He said the government is also working on expanding the mechanism to Sabah and Sarawak.
"That is what we are currently focused on," he told reporters today.
On calls by several groups, including the National Union of the Teaching Profession (NUTP), traders and transport operators, to restore the previous 300-litre subsidised fuel quota under the BUDI programme, Amir Hamzah said the government must balance subsidy spending with global supply uncertainties arising from the conflict in the Middle East.
NUTP president Aminuddin Awang said many teachers had exhausted their BUDI 95 fuel quotas earlier than expected as they use their own vehicles to carry out official duties, such as transporting students to extracurricular activities and sports events.
Amir Hamzah said more than 20 per cent of the world's oil supply passes through the Strait of Hormuz, making global fuel supply vulnerable to disruption.
However, he said Malaysia remains in a better position than many countries due to domestic oil production and the role of Petronas and other industry players in ensuring stable supply.
Although global oil prices have eased recently, he said subsidy costs remain high at between RM3.5 billion and RM4 billion a month.
He said the reduction of the subsidised quota from 300 litres to 200 litres was based on actual usage patterns under the BUDI 95 scheme, where average consumption stands at about 98 litres a month.
Amir Hamzah also outlined measures to assist micro, small and medium enterprises affected by rising costs and global uncertainties.
He said banks had agreed to discuss moratoriums and loan restructuring with affected borrowers, while the government had introduced additional financing facilities.
The Finance Ministry has provided RM5 billion in guarantees, while Bank Negara Malaysia has allocated another RM5 billion for working capital financing schemes.
Under the initiative, eligible micro, small and medium enterprises can apply for loans of up to RM750,000 for one year at an interest rate of 3.75 per cent.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MetMalaysia issues nationwide thunderstorm, heavy rain warning until 7pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain, and strong winds expected to hit nearly the entire country until 7pm today.
MetMalaysia, in a statement, said the adverse weather is forecast to occur across the whole of Perlis and Melaka.
In Kedah, the affected areas include Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, and Padang Terap, while in Perak, the areas involved are Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang, and Muallim.
For Pahang, the warning covers Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Kuantan, Pekan, and Rompin, while in Selangor, the affected areas are Hulu Selangor, Gombak, and Hulu Langat.
Meanwhile, in Negri Sembilan, the areas involved are Jelebu, Seremban, Port Dickson, Kuala Pilah, Rembau, and Tampin, while in Johor, it covers Tangkak, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, and Kulai.
In Sarawak, similar weather conditions are expected to affect Kuching, Serian, Sarikei, Sibu, Mukah, Kapit (Song, Kapit, and Bukit Mabong), Bintulu (Tatau), Miri (Miri and Marudi), and Limbang.
In Sabah, the same warning has been issued for the interior, west coast, Tawau, Sandakan (Tongod, Telupid, and Kinabatangan), and Kudat (Kota Marudu and Kudat).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Matrix Concepts unveils lifestyle hub in Kluang
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Property developer Matrix Concepts Holdings Bhd has launched X Park Bandar Seri Impian, a recreational and lifestyle destination in Kluang, Johor.
The park officially opened on Friday with a three-day event featuring sports tournaments, public activities and carnival attractions, drawing about 15,000 visitors.
Matrix Concepts said the launch marks the second X Park township collaboration after Bandar Sri Sendayan, positioning Bandar Seri Impian as another hub for recreational activities.
The launch weekend featured badminton, futsal and go-karting, as well as a pickleball clinic and LED bazaar. More than 30 schools from Kluang participated in the sports tournaments.
Matrix Concepts chairman Datuk Mohamad Haslah Mohamad Amin said X Park Bandar Seri Impian is more than a recreational facility; it serves as a community hub that brings people together through shared activities and experiences.
"This launch is a testament to our commitment to delivering engaging experiences for residents and visitors."
The park is offering free trial access to selected facilities for two weeks from May 15, including badminton, pickleball, futsal, driving range and go-karting.
The park also features ATV tracks, a gymnasium, cafes and retail outlets.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar warns allies: You betray us, we'll fight back
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today issued one of his strongest warnings yet amid growing tensions within the unity government, declaring that PH would retaliate if any party attempted to betray or undermine the coalition.
The prime minister said the Madani government had never conspired to weaken or betray its allies throughout its three years in power, despite repeated provocations and political manoeuvring behind the scenes.
"We have never incited or plotted betrayal against anyone," Anwar said during his keynote address at the 2026 PH Convention held at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre today.
He said the federal administration had deliberately slowed or adjusted certain programmes to accommodate differing views among coalition partners in the interest of preserving political stability and national unity.
"Politics must remain stable and peaceful because the country needs collective strength. Why should we break that unless we are betrayed?" he said.
However, Anwar warned that PH was fully prepared to respond if political partners chose confrontation.
"But if the drums of war are being beaten and people want to start a war, we are ready and we will fight," he said.
The Tambun member of parliament also rejected claims that PH had appeared weak by continuing to cooperate with states governed by Barisan Nasional (BN), Gabungan Parti Sarawak (GPS) and Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS).
Instead, he said the Madani administration had chosen to govern inclusively and honour federal commitments regardless of political control at state level.
"This does not mean we are weak. We struggled for decades based on principles. We negotiate properly and do not attack openly because we understand the importance of wisdom and stability," he said.
Anwar also cautioned rivals against interpreting PH's restraint as political vulnerability.
"We are not afraid of threats. We have been beaten before, jailed before and now we are supposed to be intimidated by little boys? No way," he said.
In an emotionally charged close to his speech, Anwar invoked the spirit of the Reformasi movement, rallying PH supporters to prepare for political battle if necessary.
"This is Reformasi blood. We do not back down from challenges. Fight means fight," he said.
The speech came amid escalating political friction following Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi who announced that
BN will contest all 56 seats
in the upcoming state elections.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: 80pct of UEC students sat for SPM
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) deputy chairman Anthony Loke today dismissed allegations by certain parties that the government was attempting to replace the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) through the recognition of the Unified Examination Certificate (UEC).
Loke, who is the Democratic Action Party (DAP) secretary-general, said the facts clearly showed that more than 80 per cent of UEC students also sat for the SPM examination, including 9,580 out of 10,944 students last year.
He said this had convinced the Cabinet that students from the UEC stream would continue to take the national examination as the foundation of their education.
"UEC students also sit for the SPM. The UEC is equivalent to the A-Level qualification and is used as an entry qualification for universities, but our basic requirement still requires candidates to possess an SPM certificate with at least a credit in Bahasa Melayu and a pass in History.
"This is a very clear requirement. I salute Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim for having the courage to address and resolve issues that have dragged on for decades, whereas previous prime ministers did not dare to act despite making such promises in their manifestos," he said.
Loke, who is also the Transport Minister, said this when speaking at the PH Convention at the Persada International Convention Centre in Johor today, which was also attended by Anwar and other PH leaders.
Recently, the Higher Education Ministry announced that the Cabinet had agreed on several decisions involving entry pathways for students from tahfiz institutions, private schools and Chinese Independent Secondary Schools into public institutions of higher learning (IPTA).
Loke said the policy was implemented following a Cabinet mandate, while further details involving additional subjects would be subject to decisions by university senates and the Higher Education Ministry, which is currently led by a minister from Barisan Nasional (BN).
He also assured that DAP would continue to uphold Bahasa Melayu as the national language and would never undermine its importance within the national education system.
"Bahasa Melayu will remain our national language forever. We want Malaysians to master various languages, including Malay, Chinese, English, Tamil and even Arabic. This is the philosophy of the Madani Government," he said.
Meanwhile, Loke criticised what he described as the hypocrisy of opposition leaders, particularly from Pas and Perikatan Nasional (PN), who are now opposing the move despite previously supporting UEC recognition in their manifestos.
He recalled that Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang had promoted the "Orange Book" in 2013, which included a promise to recognise the UEC, while former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin had also included similar commitments in the "Book of Hope" in 2018.
"When they wanted Chinese votes, they agreed. Now that the prime minister is implementing it, they accuse him of selling out the Malays and say it is DAP's agenda. This is hypocrisy," he said.
Commenting on criticism by MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong, who claimed that the recognition of only four UEC subjects was insufficient, Loke said that MCA had been part of the government for decades but failed to resolve the issue.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran chief negotiator Ghalibaf appointed to oversee ties with China
Article:
TEHERAN: Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who recently emerged as a chief negotiator in talks with the United States, has been appointed to oversee relations with China, Iranian media reported on Sunday.
"Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has recently been appointed as a special representative of the Islamic republic of Iran for China affairs," Tasnim news agency reported, citing "informed sources", with other media carrying similar reports.
Ghalibaf was appointed to the position at the proposal of Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and with the approval of supreme leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, according to Tasnim.
He will "coordinate various sectors of relations between Iran and China", it added.
Fars news agency said late security chief Ali Larijani, who was killed in US-Israeli strikes on March 17, held a similar position.
Larijani oversaw the advancing of negotiations with China which led to a 25-year cooperation agreement in 2021.
Following the outbreak of the war on Febraury 28 with Israel and the United States, Ghalibaf has emerged as a central figure steering high-stakes diplomacy in the single round of talks with the US in April.
A number of senior Iranian officials, including former supreme leader Ali Khamenei, were killed in the war, which spread across the Middle East before a fragile ceasefire took hold on April 8.
Iran has in recent days allowed a number of Chinese ships to pass through the strategic Strait of Hormuz, a vital global energy conduit which it had blocked since the war erupted.
The Revolutionary Guards said the ships transited after "an agreement on Iran's strait management protocols."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Amir Hamzah: IBS modular method speeds up school construction
Article:
BALIK PULAU: The use of the Industrialised Building System (IBS) modular method in school construction can reduce costs by 20 to 30 per cent and enable faster project completion.
Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the approach is being implemented under the Madani Adopted School initiative, including a pre-school facility at Sekolah Tan Sri Awang Had Salleh here.
He said the initiative aims to ensure children, particularly in rural areas, are not left behind due to inadequate educational facilities.
"We do not want our children to be marginalised or left behind. In Malaysia, especially in rural areas, children sometimes do not have sufficient facilities," he said.
"So through this adopted school project, we are building an example of what can be done in the future," he said at the launch today.
He said the project, completed within four months of groundbreaking, includes two classrooms, a dining hall, toilets, a utility room and an outdoor recreational area.
Classroom equipment, including air-conditioners, televisions, desks and chairs, was also provided under the allocation, he said.
Amir Hamzah said the IBS modular system offers advantages, including faster completion time and lower costs.
"For this school project, we estimate savings of between 20 and 30 per cent, depending on land conditions and location," he said.
"If we achieve 30 per cent savings, that is significant," he said.
He said the faster construction period would help the government meet rising demand for school places, particularly for Year One enrolment next year.
On the total cost, he said about RM900,000 was allocated under the Kampung Angkat and Sekolah Angkat programmes.
"The allocation covered two classrooms, a dining hall, toilets, a utility room, outdoor recreational facilities and classroom fittings and equipment," he said.
"Everything was completed within the allocation provided," he said.
Education Ministry secretary-general Datuk Wan Hashim Wan Rahim said the ministry is focusing on the IBS modular method for current school construction projects as it is more adaptable to existing school sites.
He said the system allows better planning to create a more complete learning environment within a defined area.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Higher education must align with National Education Policy, unity goals – Zambry
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is adopting a balanced, inclusive, and responsible approach to expanding access to higher education to build a common meeting point for Malaysia's diverse society.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said this commitment must remain aligned with efforts to uphold the foundations of the National Education Policy and National Education Philosophy.
He said the education system cannot merely be viewed through the lens of academic demands, the interests of certain groups, or narrow identity considerations.
"It also involves nation-building, strengthening unity, empowering Bahasa Melayu as the national language, and understanding Malaysia's history and nationhood.
"For this reason, the government cannot alter the foundations of the national education system simply to satisfy the demands of any particular educational stream," Zambry said.
He said this during an interview at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur today.
He was responding to a statement by the Federation of Malaysian Chinese School Management Associations (Dong Zong) on May 15, which claimed the government had failed to reflect educational justice towards Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) graduates.
Zambry described the claim as misguided and irresponsible.
He said any educational stream seeking broader access to public institutions must demonstrate a willingness to operate within the country's national framework, adding that the government must defend national principles and policies that form the basis of identity and unity.
He added that the government's decision to allow UEC holders to apply to public institutions of higher learning (IPTA) for selected Chinese language and literature courses should not be regarded as vague, unfair, or a systemic flaw as alleged by Dong Zong.
To qualify, applicants must obtain a credit in Bahasa Melayu and pass History in the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination.
On the contrary, Zambry said the move reflected an inclusive and responsible approach that respected the framework of the National Education Policy.
"In this regard, the position of Bahasa Melayu as the national language and History as the foundation of nationhood cannot be compromised. These principles must be implemented consistently and cannot change simply because of political pressure or demands from any party," he said.
Zambry said Malaysia's future depends on a common foundation capable of uniting the people through Bahasa Melayu, an understanding of the country's history, and a shared spirit of nationhood.
He added that Dong Zong needed to understand that public universities are national institutions governed by national policies, and the government has the right to impose conditions to preserve educational standards, system harmony, and long-term interests.
"The government remains committed to ensuring that the country's higher education system continues to be inclusive, fair, and of high quality. However, inclusiveness does not mean the government must sacrifice the country's core policies.
"Openness also does not mean the nation should ignore the national principles that have formed the backbone of Malaysia's development since independence," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Taiwan independence' means we don't belong to Beijing, president says
Article:
TAIPEI: The expression "Taiwan independence" means the island neither belongs to nor is subordinate to Beijing and that only the Taiwanese people can decide their future, President Lai Ching-te said on Sunday.
Lai's comments, a reiteration of his views, come days after President Donald Trump met his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping in Beijing for a summit that raised concerns in Taiwan about US backing for the island.
Trump told Fox News after the summit: "We're not looking to have somebody say, 'Let's go independent because the United States is backing us'."
China views democratically governed Taiwan as its own territory and has never renounced the use of force to bring it under Beijing's control, especially if China judges the island is seeking formal independence.
Speaking in Taipei, Lai said his Democratic Progressive Party had passed a resolution in 1999, which remains party policy, that Taiwan is already a sovereign and independent country called the Republic of China.
The government upholds that Taiwan's sovereignty cannot be violated or annexed and that its future must be decided by its people, he said.
"Everyone can clearly understand that the meaning of the term 'Taiwan independence' actually refers to Taiwan not being part of the People's Republic of China," he told an event to mark the 40th anniversary of the DPP's founding.
"It refers to the Republic of China and the People's Republic of China not being subordinate to each other," Lai added.
China's Taiwan Affairs Office did not immediately respond to a request for comment.
The defeated Republic of China government fled to Taiwan in 1949 after losing a civil war to Mao Zedong's communists, who founded the People's Republic.
Lai said that only with Taiwan can there be the Republic of China, whose territory also includes the Penghu islands in the Taiwan Strait and the Kinmen and Matsu islands which sit next to the Chinese coast.
"So it is very clear: the Republic of China has been in Taiwan for 70 or 80 years and has already become integrated with Taiwan as one," he added.
"Whether we call it the Republic of China, the Republic of China, Taiwan, or Taiwan — basically, no matter what we are called internationally, it refers to us: the 23 million people of Taiwan, Penghu, Kinmen and Matsu."
Lai did not refer to Trump in his speech nor answer questions shouted at him by reporters.
While the US has traditionally been Taiwan's most important international backer, Trump said on the flight back from Beijing that he has yet to decide on further arms sales to Taiwan, something set in US law in the 1979 Taiwan Relations Act.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China's market regulator outlines measures to support private economy
Article:
BEIJING: China's State Administration for Market Regulation has released a 2026 work plan to support the development of the private economy, outlining 34 key tasks aimed at creating a fairer market environment and improving regulatory services, reported Xinhua.
The plan calls for equal emphasis on standardised regulation and development promotion, pledging efforts to foster fair competition, innovate regulatory approaches and strengthen services to support the high-quality development of the private sector.
To reinforce legal and policy safeguards, market regulators will improve the legal framework for fair competition and the system for supervising enterprise-related charges. Efforts will be made to expand credit-based incentives, strengthen technical trade support and enhance policy measures for individual businesses, according to the plan.
The regulator also highlights measures to maintain fair market order, including removing barriers, advancing the building of a unified national market, strengthening antitrust compliance guidance and addressing rat race competition.
In terms of regulatory enforcement, authorities will explore off-site, contactless and QR-code-based inspection models, improve the fairness of administrative law enforcement, step up rectification in key areas and enhance governance capacity.
In addition, the regulator says it will strengthen services for private businesses by providing more targeted support for individual businesses, and reinforcing quality infrastructure to support high-quality development.
This policy move came as China's private companies are spearheading breakthrough innovations across various key sectors, supported by favorable government policies that encourage entrepreneurship and unleash market vitality in technological advancement.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Non-state actors are reshaping the Palestinian narrative
Article:
IN a world that often speaks of justice and human rights, Palestine remains one of the clearest litmus tests of international moral consistency.
More than seven decades on, the crisis continues without a meaningful political resolution.
The renewed attention surrounding humanitarian flotillas comes amid growing international scrutiny over humanitarian access to Gaza, increasing criticism of diplomatic paralysis and widening public frustration over the limited effectiveness of international institutions.
The movement reflects a broader pattern of citizen led mobilisation emerging in response to diplomatic paralysis and growing scepticism towards the effectiveness of international institutions.
According to Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) political analyst, Prof Datuk Dr Sivamurugan Pandian, the Global Sumud Flotilla reflects the continuing geopolitical significance of Palestine within international relations.
"The Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) reflects the persistence of the Palestine issue as a moral and political fault line in international relations. It signals frustration with diplomatic stalemate and highlights how non-state actors are stepping in to challenge perceived inaction by governments and institutions.".
Public pressure has long influenced the political environment around foreign policy. During the Vietnam War, anti war protests in the United States gained national prominence from 1965, spreading across campuses, civic groups, churches, civil rights circles and veteran networks.
Although the movement did not operate as a government, it influenced public opinion, challenged lawmakers and increased the political cost of sustaining the war.
Public opposition did not end the Vietnam War on its own. However, it became part of the wider political climate that pressured leaders to reconsider their approach.
President Richard Nixon later introduced Vietnamisation, which involved the gradual withdrawal of United States troops while transferring greater responsibility to South Vietnam.
The comparison is not exact, but it shows how sustained public mobilisation can influence the environment in which foreign policy decisions are made.
Viewed through this lens, GSF should be understood not only as a humanitarian mission, but as part of a wider pattern of citizen led mobilisation emerging during periods of diplomatic fatigue.
Its significance lies in sustaining international attention on Palestine at a time when many view formal diplomatic responses as increasingly limited.
Narrative framing remains central to how contemporary conflicts are understood internationally.
[[nid:1441645]]
Numbers may inform, but human stories create empathy and sustained public engagement.
When suffering is understood through the lived experiences, faces and voices of ordinary people, the crisis becomes harder to ignore.
Sivamurugan observed that civil society initiatives such as GSF play an increasingly important role in shaping narratives around global humanitarian crises.
Such movements, he noted, help humanise the conflict, expand international visibility and mobilise advocacy networks across borders.
In doing so, they also raise the political cost for governments perceived as passive in the face of prolonged humanitarian suffering.
The shift is particularly significant in today's media environment, where public perception is no longer shaped only by official diplomatic channels or state narratives.
Digital platforms, citizen mobilisation and transnational solidarity networks now play a central role in how global crises are understood, debated and responded to by the international community.
In the light of this, support for Palestine has increasingly moved beyond political blocs, religious identity or geography. It now involves people from different backgrounds, generations and cultures.
This growing grassroots support reflects a shift towards more decentralised and citizen driven movements. This widening role of citizen driven movements also reflects a deeper concern about the condition of global governance itself.
Former Malaysian Foreign Minister and former humanitarian special envoy, Tan Sri Dr. Syed Hamid Albar, observed that humanitarian movements such as GSF have become increasingly important at a time when many governments appear less accountable and less responsive to questions of justice, humanity and international law.
In this environment, he stressed that humanitarian organisations and civil society platforms must become more assertive to ensure that humanitarian crises such as Palestine are not forgotten or normalised through silence.
"Bodies like the Global Sumud Flotilla are important because without sustained humanitarian pressure, the cause risks fading from international attention. We must write a new narrative in order to be heard."
GSF has also moved beyond the conventional form of protest. Apart from the flotilla itself, its wider ecosystem has included parliamentary engagement, public advocacy, legal discourse, media mobilisation and international civil society coordination.
The Global Sumud Parliamentary Congress in Brussels, held on April 22, 2026, positioned the movement within a broader political track by calling attention to humanitarian access, international law and parliamentary pressure.
[[nid:1441644]]
Its stated aim included support for a UN verified humanitarian maritime corridor to Gaza. GSF 2.0 increasingly reflects a layered movement involving maritime mobilisation, parliamentary engagement, legal advocacy, media visibility and transnational solidarity networks.
This makes the movement more strategic than a single protest event. It creates multiple points of pressure through institutions, lawmakers, citizens and public conscience.
United Nations Special Rapporteur Francesca Albanese has been repeatedly cited in relation to the flotilla because of her emphasis on international law, humanitarian accountability and the need for governments to act consistently on Gaza.
During the Global Sumud Parliamentary Congress, discussions surrounding the flotilla increasingly focused on legal accountability and civilian protection.
Days later, major international media outlets reported Albanese questioning the legality of Israel intercepting and seizing flotilla vessels in international waters near Crete, reinforcing the broader legal and humanitarian concerns surrounding the mission.
"Digital connectivity and generational change are reshaping how global justice issues gain attention and momentum. GSF is part of this wider shift. It is not a centralised institution, but it has become a recognisable platform for people who want to translate concern into action."
Sivamurugan also stressed the importance of credible voices in strengthening public understanding, particularly in an environment where information moves rapidly and perception can easily overtake facts.
"Credible voices help ground the discourse. They provide context, evidence and critical analysis, moving the conversation beyond slogans towards a more informed and balanced understanding," Sivamurugan added.
GSF value lies in the pressure it creates, the debate it opens and the moral question it places before governments and institutions.
Its core message is clear: humanitarian access, justice and accountability remain global concerns, and international norms must be upheld consistently, not selectively.
* The writer is a member of the Global Sumud Flotilla Media and Communications Team
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Snap election on the table if unity pact breaks down, says Anwar
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today signalled Pakatan Harapan's (PH) readiness for a full-scale electoral showdown, saying the coalition was prepared for a snap general election should ties within the unity government deteriorate further.
The prime minister warned that PH would respond "firmly" if political rivals chose confrontation over cooperation.
"In the near future, I will hold discussions with PH leaders. If this is the way our relationship is being toyed with, perhaps we should return the mandate to the people through a nationwide general election," he said.
His remarks came amid growing tensions within the unity government following Johor BN's decision to contest all 56 state seats and DAP's indication that it was also prepared for a full contest in Negri Sembilan if needed.
Speaking at the Pakatan Harapan Convention 2026 at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here today, Anwar said discussions would soon be held with PH leadership on the coalition's readiness in Selangor, Penang and other states, following indications that Johor and Negri Sembilan were prepared for full contests.
Throughout his speech, Anwar defended the unity government's record over the past three years, saying PH had deliberately taken a cautious approach to preserve political stability and national harmony despite pressure to accelerate reforms.
The prime minister said the final decision on election timing rested with him, but stressed that any move would be made collectively after consultations with coalition leaders.
"If they fight, we will fight firmly," Anwar said, adding that PH was prepared to contest all seats in several states if politically challenged.
He also raised the prospect of wider national political developments, saying PH was seriously considering its options if attempts to destabilise relations within the unity government continued.
"Political stability is important for the country's strength. But if they choose this path, we are ready," he said.
Anwar said Johor and Negri Sembilan were "almost settled" in terms of election preparedness, declaring that both states were ready to face a full electoral battle if necessary.
Earlier, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said that if  Johor were to contest all 56 seats, Negri Sembilan would contest all 36 state seats.
"If Johor dissolves tomorrow,
Negeri Sembilan can also dissolve
tomorrow," Loke said during his address at the convention.
His remarks followed Johor Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's confirmation that
BN would contest all 56 state seat
s in the coming election.
Meanwhile, Anwar reiterated that PH was prepared to contest every seat nationwide if "the other party" opted for confrontation over cooperation in upcoming elections.
The PH chairman said the coalition would respond firmly following Johor BN's announcement that it intended to contest all 56 seats in the next Johor state election.
"We have monitored this closely and that is their decision. But if they choose to fight in Johor, PH will respond firmly and contest all seats," he said.
Anwar said PH was prepared to adopt the same approach in several states if political cooperation within the unity government was undermined.
"In Negri Sembilan, we contest all seats...  Selangor, Penang and Pahang, we also contest all seats. However, I say this if they are the ones who breach the agreement... we will be firm... If negotiations are conducted in good faith, we remain good people - patient and wise. We want to save the country because we practise stable politics.
"Johor and Negri Sembilan, we fight all. Johor ready? Negri Sembilan ready? Fight means fight," he declared to loud cheers from delegates.
The Tambun MP, however, stressed that discussions among coalition partners remained open and cordial, adding that PH still preferred political stability and consensus-building over open confrontation.
"We are good people, patient and wise. We want to save this country because we practise stable politics," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Amir Hamzah to meet Sultan of Pahang over federal allocation issues
Article:
BALIK PULAU: Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan will meet the Sultan of Pahang before the end of next week to clarify issues regarding the federal government's allocation to the state.
Amir Hamzah said the federal government remains committed to ensuring state allocations are provided in accordance with budget provisions, while efforts are also being made to increase allocations annually.
"As stated by the prime minister, I will also hold discussions with the Pahang government and meet the Sultan of Pahang before the end of next week to provide further clarification," he told reporters here today.
"Insya-Allah, when there are more important projects in terms of development expenditure, we will
increase allocations
according to existing priorities."
This follows remarks by the Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah, who reportedly called for federal government intervention in matters involving the state's revenue, including approval for river sand exports.
Amir Hamzah said it was only to ensure that all parties understood what was available, what the federal government intended to provide, and the allocations that had been given.
"Insya-Allah, this issue will be resolved," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Madani govt resolves nine MA63 matters in three years
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Madani government has achieved the best record in the nation's history by resolving nine matters under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63) within just three years of administration.
Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud said the achievement surpassed earlier administrations, which managed to resolve only four matters over a longer period.
He said 13 out of 29 MA63 claims have now been resolved through the MA63 Implementation Action Council, chaired by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"We have resolved nine matters within three years together with both state governments, with the participation of the Sarawak premier and the Sabah chief minister. This involves negotiations among the three key regions that formed Malaysia," Mustapha said.
He said this when delivering his speech as the Sabah Pakatan Harapan (PH) representative at the PH Convention 2026 at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here.
Among the key achievements were the recognition of the Public Works Department and Irrigation and Drainage Department of Sabah and Sarawak as technical departments, amendments to the Inland Revenue Board of Malaysia Act to include representatives from the two regions, and efforts to strengthen the judiciary.
Mustapha also highlighted the Madani government's commitment to raising the special interim allocation to RM600 million for each region, up from RM125.6 million in 2022.
"Where else can you find a government like this? The Madani government has not only demonstrated its commitment through words, but has also delivered on its manifesto, including appointing a deputy prime minister from Borneo for the first time in the nation's history," he said.
He added that development allocations for Sabah had increased from RM5.2 billion in 2022 to RM6.9 billion under this year's budget to accelerate critical infrastructure projects such as the Pan Borneo Highway.
Mustapha said this proved that PH and the unity government remained committed to protecting the rights of the people of Sabah and Sarawak as outlined in the party constitution and the agreement on the formation of Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Voters judging PH sincerity, warns Adam Adli at party convention
Article:
JOHOR BARU: PKR Youth chief Adam Adli urged Pakatan Harapan (PH) leaders to uphold the coalition's reformist values, warning voters were closely judging their sincerity and resolve.
PH's strength had always rested on its governance model centred on integrity, public welfare and accountability, rather than political power or personal enrichment, he said.
"We must not change our attitude and approach. We already have our own leadership model and political culture, and that is what we should continue offering to the people," he said during his debate speech representing Melaka at the Pakatan Harapan Convention 2026 at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre today.
Adam Adli's remarks come amid growing speculation over a possible snap Johor state election following viral WhatsApp messages alleging a special state assembly sitting on June 24.
The Hang Tuah Jaya member of parliament said voters were carefully assessing whether PH leaders possessed the courage, honesty and determination to govern in the people's interest amid rising economic pressures and daily challenges.
He said PH's role in government was not to pursue wealth, distribute projects or secure positions, but to administer the country responsibly and honour the mandate entrusted by the rakyat.
Adam Adli, who is also the deputy higher education minister, reminded party leaders not to neglect grassroots supporters who had long sacrificed for the coalition despite holding no positions or privileges.
He said PH's machinery in Johor and Melaka continued to rely heavily on ordinary supporters, including youths who worked during the day before carrying out party duties at night, as well as homemakers who opened their homes as operations centres while managing family responsibilities.
"These are the people who helped build our struggle and kept PH alive. They believed we would govern the country properly and not betray the
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Remand extended for man accused of abusing 5-year-old stepson
Article:
DUNGUN: A 36-year-old factory sawmill worker suspected of abusing his 5-year-old stepson has been remanded for another four days.
Magistrate Nur Amira Fatihah Osman granted the remand extension until Thursday.
Acting district police chief Deputy Superintendent Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said the extension allows police to complete investigations under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001.
He said the suspect was previously remanded for a week, a period which expired today.
"The remand extension was requested today to complete the investigation. So far, three adult witnesses and one child witness have been called to give statements," Jumaidy said.
It was earlier reported that the man was arrested following a report lodged by the victim's biological mother on May 10 regarding the alleged abuse.
The victim's 30-year-old mother, who is married to the suspect, discovered the abuse on April 7 after noticing burn marks on her son's fingers.
Jumaidy said the victim informed his mother that the suspect used a lighter to burn the ring finger on his left hand because he was naughty, an act the suspect admitted to.
"The victim also complained to his older sister that the suspect had stomped on his stomach and choked him, though there were no eyewitnesses to those incidents.
"Investigations revealed that the suspect, who has two children, married the victim's mother, who also has two children, three years ago," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Rafizi, Nik Nazmi to leave PKR, vacate parliamentary seats
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Pandan and Setiawangsa members of arliament Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad today announced that they will vacate their respective seats, effective tomorrow.
The duo said they would write to Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul to formally notify him of their decision.
"I will officially write to the speaker tomorrow to inform him of my decision to resign as MP and vacate the Pandan parliamentary seat," Rafizi said at an event today.
Nik Nazmi followed suit, confirming that he would also vacate his parliamentary seat.
"I hereby announce that I will vacate the Setiawangsa parliamentary seat effective tomorrow," he said.
The duo also announced that they are quitting PKR and will join Parti Bersama Malaysia, effective Tuesday.
They said they would submit their formal resignation letters to PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh on Tuesday.
"We are not the best among all of us, we know that. We have many weaknesses and many shortcomings.
"But right now, we have been entrusted with the responsibility of carrying the torch of this idealism while we still have the strength, so that one day we can pass that torch on to the leadership of the younger generation,"
Rafizi said.
Together with Nik Nazmi, and their colleagues, they will devote all our energy to shaping and training this new generation of young leaders from within, he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: JS-SEZ demonstrates Madani govt's economic gains in Johor - Saifuddin Nasution
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The development of the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) is proof of the effectiveness of the Madani Government's strategy in translating economic prosperity into direct benefits for the state and its people.
Pakatan Harapan (PH) secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the unique project, resulting from cooperation between the two countries, has successfully attracted major investments, accounting for 66 per cent of Johor's total investments over the past year.
He said to support the growth of the economic region, the government is placing strong emphasis on connectivity and mobility at the country's entry points to ensure it is no longer constrained by outdated management systems.
"We are replacing outdated border management systems, expediting procurement programmes, building new systems, and putting in place the most advanced infrastructure. We are now just awaiting their phased implementation," he said in his speech at the PH Convention 2026 at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here today.
Saifuddin, who is also home minister, said efforts to address congestion at the Johor Causeway, Bangunan Sultan Iskandar and Kompleks Sultan Abu Bakar are now a priority for the Cabinet committee to ensure the smooth movement of people and goods.
"The Johor-Singapore entry point is currently among the busiest in the world, so we can no longer be bound by outdated systems or old methods of managing it," he said.
Meanwhile, he said Johor was chosen as the venue for the convention to reinvigorate the coalition machinery, as the state is among those expected to face elections in the near future.
He added that all the issues and messages raised by delegates during the convention would serve as a guide for the Presidential Council in determining the coalition's strategy and political direction.
He also reminded party machinery to play an active role in conveying accurate information about government policies to the public to prevent any gap between the administration's achievements and understanding at the grassroots level.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cardiac anaesthesiologist finds new peace in farming
Article:
BACHOK: If her life was once filled with pressure and the adrenaline of saving patients in the operating theatre, a former cardiac anaesthesiologist at Universiti Sains Malaysia Specialist Hospital (HPUSM) has now found a new sense of peace in sheep and goat farming.
Dr Saedah Ali, 59, who began her career as a medical doctor in 1992 and was involved in pioneering cardiac surgery services at HPUSM in 2001, ventured into livestock farming in June 2018, about four years before taking optional retirement in 2022.
She said her interest in the field was sparked after seeing her late father's 1-hectare (2.5-acre) inherited land left overgrown with bushes and undergrowth, prompting her to develop it into a livestock farm as a side activity.
"At first, I only rented out the land to villagers for about RM100 a year, but over time, I developed the idea of venturing into livestock farming, as it is also considered a sunnah.
"There is a form of communication and emotional connection when raising livestock. Although it can be exhausting, especially when I have to stay up at night when the animals are sick or giving birth, it has actually become a form of therapy to relieve work-related stress," she told Bernama recently.
According to her, the stress of being an anaesthesiologist is very different from the stress of managing livestock.
Starting with just eight goats, the farm she runs has since grown to include about 250 sheep and 90 goats.
Dr Saedah said she focuses on the Dorper breed, a cross between Dorset and Persian Blackhead sheep that is known as a premium meat breed, with nearly 90 per cent of the breeding stock imported from Australia.
Besides sheep, she also raises Boer goats and Boer crossbreeds for meat production. To ensure operating costs remain efficient and sustainable, she prioritises the use of natural feed sources by reducing dependence on bran and pellets.
"More than 70 per cent of the livestock feed at this farm consists of Napier grass and Bermuda grass grown on-site, in addition to silage, which is fermented grass," she said.
However, she acknowledged that the local livestock industry is currently facing various challenges, including an influx of cheap imported goats that has affected local breeders.
According to her, some of the imported livestock are believed to consist of older or lower-quality goats, but they continue to attract demand due to their lower prices.
"Over the past three to four years, many local breeders have been forced to shut down because operating costs can no longer be sustained by current selling prices.
"Besides that, we are also facing rising feed prices due to global geopolitical factors, as well as weather-related challenges such as droughts and floods," she said.
In this regard, she hopes the government and relevant agencies will intervene by introducing certain controls on the entry of imported supplies to protect the local livestock industry and strengthen the country's food security.
Besides generating income, Dr Saedah's farm also contributes to the local community through a Corporate Social Initiative (CSI) collaboration with the prison authorities by providing employment opportunities to parolees over the past two years.
According to her, the farm also frequently serves as a practical training centre for students from various educational institutions, including Universiti Malaysia Kelantan, Universiti Putra Malaysia, Universiti Sultan Zainal Abidin, vocational colleges and the Institut Pertanian Titi Gantung in Perak.
Her success in livestock farming has also been supported by her husband, who is an ear, nose and throat (ENT) specialist at HPUSM and often helps treat sick livestock after working hours.
Concluding the interview, Dr Saedah advised professionals to engage in outdoor activities or hobbies to avoid excessive work-related stress.
"Professionals greatly need outdoor activities to avoid burnout. Whether it is gardening, fishing or raising livestock, find an activity that you enjoy so that when retirement comes, you can remain active and healthy," she said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: SAR ends after second drowned sibling found in Kota Belud river
Article:
KOTA BELUD: Search and rescue (SAR) operations for two siblings who drowned in a river near a campsite in Polumpung Melangkap Baru have ended following the recovery of the second body.
The victim, 18-year-old Daniel Abdullah, was found by members of the public at 12.04pm today, roughly 200m from where he had been swimming.
District Fire and Rescue Station chief Elge Bakuk said the second day of SAR operations began at 8.50am, with the search split into three sectors.
Sector A covered a 3.4km stretch along the river from Polumpung Melangkap to Sondot View Camp, while Sector B spanned 5.2km from SK Narinang to Sondot View Camp.
"Meanwhile, non-governmental organisation Team Rafting carried out search efforts in Sector C using rafts, starting from the incident location to Pekan Taginambur," Elge said when contacted.
He added that the victim's body was handed over to the police at 12.30pm for further action.
The SAR operation was officially called off at 1.30pm after rescue teams ensured the scene was entirely secure.
The operation involved 13 personnel from the Kota Belud Fire and Rescue Station, assisted by the police, Health Ministry, Civil Defence Force, and Team Rafting.
In the
incident yesterday
, the victim's older brother, Zul Hakim Abdullah, 23, was found drowned by firefighters at 10.18am, about 800m from the scene.
Zul Hakim drowned after he attempted to rescue Daniel, who was believed to have been swept away by strong currents while taking a dip in the river.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas lacks other capable leaders, Guan Eng says after Samsuri's appointment
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP adviser Lim Guan Eng has questioned Pas' decision to appoint Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar as Opposition Leader while he continues serving as Terengganu Menteri Besar, saying the move places him under two heavy responsibilities simultaneously.
Lim, the former DAP secretary-general, said Samsuri, who is also the Kemaman member of parliament and Ru Rendang assemblyman, would now have to hold two major positions at the same time.
"The question arises whether Pas has no capable leader apart from Ahmad Samsuri to shoulder these two responsibilities by wearing 'two hats' as Opposition Leader and Terengganu Menteri Besar.
"Both positions carry heavy duties and responsibilities. Either one alone would require full focus and commitment. Perhaps Ahmad Samsuri himself may also be confused about which role he should prioritise, whether as Opposition Leader or Menteri Besar of Terengganu," he said.
Lim also raised practical concerns over Samsuri's ability to effectively carry out both roles, given the distance between Kuala Lumpur and Kuala Terengganu, as well as the demanding nature of both positions.
He referred to Samsuri's attendance record in the Dewan Rakyat and questioned whether the Kemaman MP could properly fulfil both responsibilities when the two work locations were so far apart.
"If he attends Parliament every sitting day, would he still be able to fully carry out his duties as Menteri Besar while being absent from Kuala Terengganu?" he asked.
Lim said it was unrealistic and impractical for Samsuri to wear "two hats" while effectively discharging both responsibilities.
"In the end, the people of Terengganu and voters who supported PN at the parliamentary level will lose out because they will not receive his full attention and focus. It appears Pas truly has no other credible leader apart from Ahmad Samsuri," he said.
Yesterday, Perikatan Nasional secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan announced that Samsuri had been appointed the new Opposition Leader following a PN Supreme Council meeting chaired by him.
The position became vacant after Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin, the Larut member of parliament and Bersatu deputy president, reportedly resigned from the role last month after being expelled from the party in February.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nepali duo break own records on Everest
Article:
KATHMANDU: A Nepali climber dubbed the "Everest Man", Kami Rita Sherpa, scaled Mount Everest for a record 32nd time Sunday, while Lhakpa Sherpa broke her own women's record with an 11th summit.
"This is another milestone in Nepal's mountaineering history," Himal Gautam, spokesperson for Nepal's Tourism Department, told AFP.
Kami Rita Sherpa, 56, first stood on the top of Mount Everest in 1994 while working for a commercial expedition.
Since then, he has climbed Everest almost every year, guiding clients.
Lhakpa Sherpa, 52, known as the "Mountain Queen", first stood on the top of Everest in 2000, becoming the first Nepali woman to successfully summit and descend the world's highest peak.
"Their record gives greater excitement to other climbers," Gautam added.
"By breaking records through healthy competition on Everest, will help make climbing safer, more dignified, and better managed."
Kami Rita Sherpa, speaking in 2024, after another ascent of the 8,849-metre (29,032-foot) peak said that he was "just working" and did not plan on setting records.
A climbing boom has made mountaineering a lucrative business since Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay Sherpa made the first ascent in 1953.
Nepal has issued a record 492 Everest permits this season, with a city of tents set up at the foot of Everest for climbers and support staff.
As most mountaineers attempt the ascent with the help of at least one Nepali guide, about a thousand climbers are expected to head for the summit in the next few days.
The high numbers have rekindled concerns about overcrowding on the mountain, especially if poor weather shortens the climbing window.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stay ready during Southwest Monsoon, CDF chief tells personnel
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia Civil Defence Force (CDF) personnel have been urged to remain prepared for any eventualities during the current Southwest Monsoon phase.
CDF chief commissioner Mohammad Abiddin Abd Rahman said they must remain adaptive and proactive by leveraging their training and experience to ensure a swift response when needed.
"I am confident that various preparatory measures have been taken by all personnel, including conducting a range of training programmes for members and local communities to reduce disaster risks to lives and property," he said.
He said this while speaking at the Federal Territories (FT) CDF assembly here today, which was also attended by FT Civil Defence director Mohd Rosman Abdullah.
The Southwest Monsoon is reported to have begun on May 14 and is expected to continue until September, following the onset of consistent winds blowing from the southwest towards the region.
Recently, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) director-general Mohd Hisham Mohd Anip reportedly said that during this period, lower humidity levels typically result in reduced cloud formation in most areas.
This condition will lead to lower rainfall distribution across the country compared to other periods, with more dry days than rainy days.
Meanwhile, Abiddin urged CDF personnel to remain resilient and committed in carrying out their responsibilities and daily duties in service of religion, nation and country.
"I also hope that we will continue to enhance our skills and knowledge, while strengthening our integrity and team spirit. We are not merely a rescue force, but also a pillar of national resilience trusted by the people," he said.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Negri Umno, BN yet to discuss state polls preparations
Article:
SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Umno-Barisan Nasional (BN) has not held any discussions regarding preparations or actions for the upcoming state election.
State Umno liaison secretary Datuk Mustapha Nagoor said the party has not made any decisions concerning the polls, including the seats it will contest.
"For now, there are no discussions regarding the state election. We are still within the unity government in Negri Sembilan," he said when contacted today.
Mustapha was asked to comment on whether Negri Sembilan Umno-BN intends to contest all 36 state seats in the upcoming election, following Johor BN's recent move.
Yesterday, Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced that the coalition would contest all 56 state seats in its next election, noting the decision took into account the views of Johoreans and grassroots leaders.
In the 2023 Negri Sembilan state election, Umno-BN collaborated with Pakatan Harapan (PH) and won 14 state seats.
The two coalitions subsequently formed the state government after securing a two-thirds majority by winning a total of 31 state seats.
Perikatan Nasional, comprising Pas and Bersatu, won only five state seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Century-old apam business earns Michelin Bib Gourmand honour
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: For nearly four hours each day, 65-year-old R. Neelamegan stands over open flames, enduring intense heat without complaint, his determination unshaken as he carries on his family's apam manis business, a legacy that has endured for more than a century.
Operating from a coffee shop on Jalan Burma here,
Ravi's Claypot Appom Manis
stall is often packed with both locals and foreign tourists as soon as it opens at 6am, with some even willing to queue to get a taste of its
apam manis
, a delicacy rarely found elsewhere.
Using charcoal as fuel and clay pots imported from India, the traditional cooking method gives the
apam
a distinctive aroma that has become its signature, making it a daily breakfast favourite passed down through generations.
Neelamegan, better known as Ravi among customers, said his father N. Rajagopal, who came from Tamil Nadu, India, started the
apam manis
business in 1922, alongside the opening of the coffee shop, which then charged a rent of just half a cent.
"I am from the second generation and took over the business from my brother about 35 years ago, and we have never moved from this location since my late father started it more than 100 years ago," he told Bernama recently.
The father of five said that although modern methods now allow
apam
to be cooked using gas stoves and ordinary pans, he continues to use charcoal and clay pots to preserve the original aroma and taste.
Ravi, who is fluent in Hokkien, said the recipe has been slightly adapted over time but still uses key ingredients such as rice flour, sugar, coconut milk and eggs.
"The rice flour batter is prepared by hand the night before to ensure it rises properly. Early in the morning at the stall, I then mix in eggs, sugar and fresh coconut milk bought from a nearby market," he said, adding that they use around three to four kg of flour each day and can sell between 500 and 700 pieces of
apam
, with slightly higher sales on weekends.
Ravi said the biggest challenge is controlling the charcoal embers, which requires skill and stamina, as well as standing for long hours in the heat while preparing the
apam
.
He added that the
apam manis
is more than just a traditional dish, describing it as a quiet symbol of unity in Penang's multi-ethnic community, where it is enjoyed daily by customers from all walks of life.
Ravi said one of his fondest memories from running the business was being invited on several occasions to the Pahang palace to prepare
apam
.
The business is now being passed on to his fourth child, 30-year-old N. Balasivan, who will continue the family's
apam
-making legacy into the next generation.
Balasivan said the traditional
apam
-making method has also been recognised, with the business receiving a Michelin Bib Gourmand award last year.
"After receiving the recognition, I felt even more responsible for continuing the business and preserving its tradition and authenticity, especially as fewer people now use clay pots and we even have to bring them in from India," he said.
Meanwhile, Sophie Goh, 53, from Tanjung Bungah and a regular customer at the stall for more than 20 years, said she grew up enjoying Ravi's
apam manis
, which she described as creamier, softer and fluffier than apam she has tried elsewhere.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: Negri Sembilan can dissolve assembly 'tomorrow'
Article:
JOHOR BARU: DAP Negri Sembilan is ready to mirror Johor's political timeline, said DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke today.
The Seremban member of parliament said Negri Sembilan could dissolve its assembly "tomorrow" if Johor takes the lead.
He said that if Barisan Nasional (BN) contests all 56 seats in Johor, PH would similarly contest all 36 seats in Negri Sembilan.
Yesterday, Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said it will
contest all 56 state seats
in the coming election.
The menteri besar said the decision followed discussions with the coalition's top leadership and grassroots leaders.
He described the move as a clear commitment to preserve political stability and ensure continued development in Johor.
Loke said DAP Negri Sembilan has signalled full readiness to enter the election arena.
"If Johor contests all 56 seats, Negri Sembilan will contest 36. If Johor dissolves tomorrow, Negri Sembilan can also dissolve tomorrow," he said during his address at the Pakatan Harapan Convention 2026 at the Persada Johor International Convention Centre here today.
"Negri Sembilan is prepared to mirror Johor's political timeline, including the possibility of dissolving its state assembly 'tomorrow' if Johor takes the lead," he said.
He said DAP was now in "gelanggang" mode, stressing that the coalition had moved into full campaign preparedness ahead of potential state elections.
Loke's remarks come amid early political positioning by both government and opposition blocs, as well as Johor's BN-led administration, with parties beginning to signal readiness for possible simultaneous or closely timed state elections.
Johor has 56 state assembly seats, while Negri Sembilan has 36.
Although smaller, Negri Sembilan carries considerable strategic weight in national political calculations.
Loke also used the platform to highlight PH's governance narrative, particularly federal-level infrastructure and transport projects in Johor, which he said had contributed significantly to the state's recent economic growth.
He cited key connectivity projects, including the Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link between Johor Baru and Singapore, which is nearing completion and expected to begin operations in January next year.
Loke said the RTS project, along with upgrades to rail and freight connectivity, had been made possible through cooperation between several federal ministries under the unity government, adding that it would transform Johor Baru into a modern economic hub.
He also pointed to the expansion of rail services, including the Gemas–Johor Baru electrified double-tracking project, which now allows Electric Train Service (ETS) trains to run from the north of the peninsula directly to Johor Baru.
He highlighted improvements in freight movement from Pasir Gudang to Tanjung Pelepas Port, as well as plans to position Senai Airport as a key aviation hub in southern Peninsular Malaysia.
He said development in Johor was not driven by a single level of government but by coordinated federal-state efforts, dismissing claims that progress in the state was one-sided.
"Facts must be brought to the ground. When we enter the election field, these are the realities that must be explained to voters," he said.
He said that PH's governance approach was based on inclusivity and performance, and urged party machinery to rely on data and delivery records when engaging voters ahead of the polls.
Any dissolution of either state assembly would pave the way for fresh state elections, which are typically held within 60 days after dissolution.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Khaled says government to assess new opposition leader Samsuri
Article:
MUAR: The government will monitor the performance of Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar in his new role as opposition leader, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said.
It was too early to evaluate Samsuri as the appointment was only announced yesterday, he said.
He said the government would observe and assess performance in all positions, including the opposition leader.
It was too early to evaluate
Samsuri as the appointment
had only just been announced, he said.
Samsuri, who is Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman and Terengganu menteri besar, will replace Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin as opposition leader.
Hamzah was
sacked from Bersatu
in February.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Rafizi, Nik Nazmi take over Parti Bersama Malaysia
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and former natural resources and environmental sustainability minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad today took over Parti Bersama Malaysia.
This was announced at an event here today, where the party's founder and president, Tan Gin Theam, presented a mock party registration certificate to Rafizi and Nik Nazmi to hand over the leadership of the party and officiate the transition.
The party, also known as the Malaysian United Party, was established in 2016.
Also present were several members of parliament, namely Wong Chen (Subang), Datuk Bakhtiar Wan Chik (Balik Pulau), Rodziah Ismail (Ampang), and Zahir Hassan (Wangsa Maju).
Meanwhile, Nik Nazmi, in his speech, said the existing political system remains trapped in a cycle of instability and compromise, pushing aside the reform agenda and changes that have been promised time and again.
"Every day, people ask: what has happened? Why have Pakatan Harapan and PKR become like this? Why are their supporters being taken for granted? Why is PH no longer listening to the people?
"Why has PH betrayed the very principles it once stood for? Why are the same mistakes being repeated when voters chose them precisely to correct those failures?
"These are difficult questions to answer."
He said PH once spoke the language of hope; today, it increasingly relies on the politics of fear.
"In urban areas, people are quietly told that if PH is not elected, Pas will come to power.
"What worries Malaysians most, especially the younger generation, is when national leaders continue to equivocate while promises of reform are repeatedly delayed."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pua questions PH-BN pact after Johor seat move
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP's Tony Pua has urged Pakatan Harapan (PH) to begin planning an exit from the unity government, warning that the bloc risks being "stabbed in the back" by Umno.
The former Damansara member of parliament said Umno's decision to contest all 56 Johor state seats in the upcoming state election had exposed the party's "true colours".
"By declaring their intent to contest all 56 state seats in Johor in the upcoming state elections, Umno has revealed its true colours, wanting its cake and eating it too.
"This makes an electoral pact between Harapan and Barisan Nasional in GE16 completely untenable.
"It is time to set a timeline to dissolve the Madani coalition, lest we be stabbed in the back by the treacherous Umno," he said in a Facebook post.
He said PH had done everything possible to maintain political stability under the Unity government, despite what he described as an "awkward arrangement" with BN following the 2022 general election.
He said sacrifices had been made in the interest of stability, which he acknowledged had benefited the country.
However, he said the arrangement with Umno should not be taken for granted, adding that there was little reason to trust assurances from Umno leaders that Johor BN's move would not affect federal ties.
"They cannot be trusted," he said.
He said PH must be prepared for the possibility of contesting future elections independently, despite potential challenges and negative voter perceptions.
He said it would be better for PH to lose with dignity than to continue facing what he described as political betrayal.
Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi yesterday announced that BN would contest all
56 state seats
in the upcoming Johor election, a move widely seen as an early indication of BN's intention to strengthen its position ahead of GE16.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Nearly 60pct of Malaysian workers overweight or obese
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Nearly 60 per cent of Malaysian workers who underwent health screenings were found to be overweight or obese, revealing a worrying health trend in the workforce.
The findings were based on an analysis of the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) Health Screening Programme (HSP) from 2023 to 2025.
Deputy Human Resources Minister Datuk Khairul Firdaus Akbar Khan said the 59.2 per cent figure was significantly high and could not be taken lightly.
"The data also showed that about 19 per cent of them suffer from diabetes, while 17.45 per cent have high blood pressure.
"59.24 per cent of those screened have high cholesterol, with this risk profile concentrated among workers aged between 40 and 59," he said.
He said this during the launch of the Perkeso Run and Ride 2026 in conjunction with Labour Day at Dataran Wawasan today.
Also present were Human Resources Ministry deputy secretary-general (policy and international) and acting secretary-general Dr Mohd Shaharin Umar, Youth and Sports Ministry secretary-general Datuk Rahimi Ismail, Human Resources Ministry deputy secretary-general (operations) Sutekno Ahmad Belon, Perkeso chairman Datuk Seri Subahan Kamal and Perkeso group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Dr Mohammed Azman Aziz Mohammed.
Khairul Firdaus said the public must realise that the pre-retirement phase is a highly productive yet critical period for non-communicable disease (NCD) risks.
"In fact, the Health Ministry has revealed that only 14.7 per cent of senior citizens in Malaysia currently achieve healthy ageing," he said.
He also expressed concern over cancer, which remains the third-highest cause of death in Malaysia and is becoming increasingly common among younger people.
He urged the public to take the dangers of NCDs seriously, using their participation in the event as an intervention toward a healthy lifestyle and balanced nutrition.
"I am proud of the positive momentum shown today, as participants in their 40s and 50s represent nearly 30 per cent of the total turnout for the Perkeso Run and Ride 2026.
"This is a very good development, and I hope all workers, regardless of age, will continue to practise a healthy lifestyle.
"The health of our workers determines our productivity and directly influences our socioeconomic sustainability," he said.
The ninth edition of the event drew its largest turnout yet with 12,000 participants.
This year's edition expanded the running categories to 3km, 6km and 12km to encourage active participation, alongside the traditional 35km fun ride.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Elderly Kedah school bus driver turns to funeral transport work after losing students, vehicle seized by JPJ
Article:
BALING, May 17 — A 73-year-old school bus driver in Kedah was forced to turn his vehicle into a paid passenger transport service after struggling to find students to ferry over the past two years, leading to the bus being seized by the Road Transport Department (JPJ) for allegedly misusing its permit.
The school bus was detained during an operation by Kedah JPJ at the Gurun toll exit near here today after it was found carrying 26 passengers from Taman Intan, Sungai Petani, to Bukit Jenun, Pendang, to attend a funeral ceremony.
Kedah JPJ director Stien Van Lutam said investigations found that the bus had not been transporting schoolchildren for the past two years due to a lack of demand.
“The situation led the driver, who is also the bus owner, to take up paid passenger transport services from one destination to another,” he said during a press conference at the Kuala Ketil JPJ Enforcement Station today.
Although the vehicle had valid insurance coverage and road tax, Van Lutam said it had breached the conditions of its operator’s licence by carrying passengers other than school students.
According to him, the bus was seized under Section 80(1) of the Land Public Transport Act 2010 for allegedly committing an offence under Section 22(3) of the same Act.
“The bus fulfilled all requirements, including having valid insurance and road tax, but it violated the operator’s licence conditions by carrying passengers other than school students,” he said.
The elderly driver was also brought in to have his statement recorded.
Meanwhile, Van Lutam said the Kedah JPJ is intensifying several enforcement operations to reduce road accidents and improve compliance among motorists in the state ahead of the festive season.
He said the Hari Raya Aidiladha Special Operation 2026 would run from May 24 to 31, with monitoring and enforcement activities focusing on strategic locations and accident-prone areas.
At the same time, the Kedah JPJ is continuing Op Luxury, which targets luxury vehicle owners found violating road regulations.
“From July 1 last year until now, a total of 23 luxury vehicles have been seized in Kedah through the operation,” he said.
Among the vehicles seized were high-end models such as the Porsche Cayenne, Mini Countryman and Mercedes-Benz A250 Sport. — Bernama

Title: Amir Hamzah to seek audience with Pahang Sultan this week over federal allocation concerns
Article:
BALIK PULAU, May 17 — Finance Minister II Amir Hamzah Azizan will seek an audience with the Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah in the near future to clarify issues regarding the Federal Government’s allocation to the state of Pahang.
Finance Minister II Amir Hamzah Azizan said the Federal Government’s commitment is clear in ensuring that every allocation to the state is made based on needs and the Federal Constitution.
“I will seek an audience with the Sultan of Pahang, God willing, this week to clarify a little and this is to ensure that all parties understand what we have given and the allocations provided
“I don’t think there is a problem with this matter,” he told reporters after attending the Opening and Handing Over Ceremony of the Additional Block of Preschool Classes of the Special Education Integration Programme (PPKI) at Sekolah Kebangsaan Tan Sri Awang Had Salleh here today.
He explained that the government always strives to increase allocations to the state from time to time, especially involving development spending based on current priorities.
On May 8, the Tengku Mahkota of Pahang Tengku Hassanal Ibrahim Alam Shah, while officiating the Opening Ceremony of the First Meeting of the Fifth Term of the 15th Pahang State Legislative Assembly, questioned the Federal Government’s allocations to the state.
In this regard, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he would ask Amir Hamzah to provide a comprehensive explanation regarding the Federal Government’s allocations to the state of Pahang to be submitted to the Tengku Mahkota of Pahang. — Bernama

Title: Anwar signals early polls if unity government pressures persist
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, May 17 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim warned political partners in national unity government today that he is prepared to call for an early general election if their political "intimidation" and threats persist.
Speaking at the 2026 Pakatan Harapan Convention here, Anwar, who is the PH chairman, directly referenced the ongoing state-level tensions, escalating the situation to a national scale.
“If it is true that they feel it is time for the election to take place in Johor, we will continue in Negeri Sembilan, and perhaps the people can decide for the whole of Malaysia,” he said in his keynote address.
MORE TO COME

Title: Zambry: Higher education access must align with national policy, unity goals, stresses BM and History requirements for UEC holders
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 17 — The government is adopting a balanced, inclusive and responsible approach in its efforts to expand access to national higher education as part of building a common meeting point for Malaysia’s diverse society.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir said the commitment must nevertheless remain aligned with efforts to uphold the foundations of the National Education Policy and the National Education Philosophy.
According to him, in a sovereign nation, the education system cannot merely be viewed through the lens of academic demands, the interests of certain groups or narrow identity considerations.
“It also involves nation-building, strengthening unity, empowering Bahasa Melayu as the national language, as well as understanding Malaysia’s history and nationhood.
“For this reason, the government cannot alter the foundations of the national education system simply to satisfy the demands of any particular educational stream,” he told Bernama in an exclusive interview at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur today.
He was responding to a statement by the Federation of Malaysian Chinese School Management Associations (Dong Zong) on May 15, which claimed that the government had failed to reflect educational justice towards Unified Examination Certificate (UEC) graduates.
Zambry described the claim as misguided and irresponsible.
